codeReview.summary: The patch adds the intended `currentMessageId` seeding helper and solid happy-path unit coverage, but it still leaves a selection-time race because `selectScoop()` performs one SessionStore read for seeding and `ChatPanel.switchToContext()` performs a second read for hydration. Those reads can observe different snapshots while the offscreen bridge is asynchronously persisting mid-stream events, so the panel can hydrate a streaming assistant message that `currentMessageId` never learns about and the next delta can still fork.

issues.description: `selectScoop()` now awaits `client.reconcileForScoopSelection(scoop)` before `layout.panels.chat.switchToContext(...)`, but the helper does its own `SessionStore.load()` (`packages/webapp/src/ui/offscreen-client.ts:99-125`) and `switchToContext()` performs a second independent `sessionStore.load()` (`packages/webapp/src/ui/chat-panel.ts:159-176`). Because bridge persistence is fire-and-forget on every delta/tool event (`.factory/library/architecture.md:144-148`), these two reads can observe different snapshots. If the first read misses a just-persisted in-progress assistant message and the second read hydrates it, `currentMessageId` stays null even though the panel now shows that message, so `handleAgentEvent()` still creates a fresh row on the next `text_delta` (`packages/webapp/src/ui/offscreen-client.ts:409-416`). The new tests only call `reconcileForScoopSelection()` directly and do not exercise the real `selectScoop()`/`switchToContext()` sequence, so this race remains uncovered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `webapp-worker`'s manual-verification rules conflict with this feature's explicit guidance. The skill says any feature whose description mentions UI/panel behavior requires manual CDP verification and skipped checks must set `followedProcedure: false`, but this feature description marks manual verification OPTIONAL; the worker skipped browser checks, left `interactiveChecks` empty, and still reported `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:89-96` requires manual verification for user-observable UI behavior; `features.json:1830` marks manual CDP verification OPTIONAL for this feature; the handoff `2026-04-18T05-58-14-593Z__misc-reconcile-offscreen-client-currentmessageid-on-selection__aef240b9-2b7c-48c1-bee0-cd9ad524d30b.json:12,51,82` records the skip with `interactiveChecks: []` and `followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail. The implementation improves the seeded-message happy path, but it still relies on a pre-hydration SessionStore read that can diverge from the later `switchToContext()` hydration snapshot, so the original message-forking bug can still happen under live mid-stream persistence timing. The added tests cover helper-level seeding/clearing, not the real selection flow that introduces the remaining race.
